title: Lev Fernandez — Founder & CEO, Vellum Systems
compiled_truth:
Lev Fernandez is the founder and CEO of [Vellum Systems](companies/vellum-systems), an AI infrastructure company he started in 2024 after stints at several infrastructure and developer-tools startups. He's known internally and in the broader infra community as a long-term thinker — the kind of person who builds five-year roadmaps before writing the first line of code. Colleagues describe him as deeply analytical, sometimes to a fault; he's been known to delay product decisions by weeks while he models second-order effects on adoption curves.

Before founding Vellum, Lev spent roughly six years bouncing between mid-stage infrastructure companies, mostly in roles that blurred the line between engineering and product strategy. He never stayed anywhere longer than two years, which he's openly acknowledged was a weakness early on. "I kept seeing the same architectural mistakes repeated," he said during a 2027 podcast appearance, "and I figured the only way to fix it was to start from scratch." That impulse became [Vellum Systems](companies/vellum-systems), which focuses on inference-layer infrastructure for AI workloads — essentially the plumbing between model outputs and downstream systems.

Fernandez has been a quiet but persistent presence in the deterministic infrastructure discourse. He was an early voice arguing that AI inference pipelines need the same kind of rigor that financial systems demand — reproducibility, auditability, bitwise consistency across environments. This philosophical alignment brought him into contact with the Anchor Codec team around mid-2027, initially through a shared panel at a small infrastructure confernece in Denver. The relationship has been exploratory so far; Vellum's inference orchestration layer could theoretically benefit from single-pass primitives, but no formal integration work has been announced.

Lev is based in Chicago, which he chose deliberately to stay outside the gravitational pull of the coasts. He runs a small team — roughly 30 people as of late 2027 — and has been deliberate about keeping headcount low relative to the capital Vellum has raised. He's said publicly that he views premature hiring as a form of technical debt, which tracks with his general disposition toward caution and long-horizon planning. Those who've worked with him note that he's not flashy, rarely tweets, and prefers dense written memos to slide decks. His analytical bent sometimes reads as cold, but people who know him well say he's just unusually focused on getting the architecture right before anything else.
timeline:
- **2026-03-10** | Vellum Systems closes a seed extension round; Lev Fernandez posts a brief memo on AI inference reliability to the company blog, attracting attention from infrastructure engineers.
- **2026-08-22** | Fernandez gives a talk at a Chicago developer meetup on "Why Inference Pipelines Are the New Databases" — one of his first public speaking appearances.
- **2026-11-04** | Hires Priya Okonkwo as VP of Engineering at [Vellum Systems](companies/vellum-systems), signaling a shift toward building out the core platform team.
- **2027-02-15** | Publishes a long-form essay arguing that non-deterministic outputs in AI systems create hidden compliance risks; the piece circulates widely in infra circles.
- **2027-06-09** | Participates in a panel at the Denver Infrastructure Summit alongside members of the Anchor Codec team; first known contact between the two organizations.
- **2027-07-28** | Internal meeting between Lev and Anchor's partnerships lead to discuss potential integration surface areas between Vellum's orchestration layer and Anchor's single-pass primitives.
- **2027-10-12** | [Vellum Systems](companies/vellum-systems) announces Series A; Fernandez emphasizes in interviews that the round was intentionally kept small to preserve optionality.
- **2028-01-19** | Lev attends a private dinner organized by infrastructure founders in New York; reportedly spent most of the evening in a deep conversation about authentication at the inference boundary.
- **2028-04-03** | Vellum ships v2.0 of their orchestration platform, which includes early support for pluggable verification modules — widely interpreted as a nod toward deterministic infra partnerships.
- **2028-06-17** | Fernandez quoted in a trade publication calling deterministic data infrastructure "the missing layer" in production AI systems.

_facts.name: Lev Fernandez
_facts.title: Founder & CEO, Vellum Systems
_facts.background: Founded Vellum Systems in 2024; previously at infrastructure and developer-tools companies
